title: Ternary liquid equilibria: Water‐fatty acid‐solvent systems
author_string: Rao, M. Raja, Rao, C. Venkata
abstract: AbstractPhase equilibrium data are presented for ternary liquid systems with propionic acid as solute, and with n‐hexane, cyclohexane, cyclohexene, toluene and tetrachloroethylene, respectively, as solvents. Propionic acid is found to favour the water phase in all the systems. Except at higher acid concentrations, the solvents ‘hexane’ b.p. 67–69°, cyclohexane and tetrachloroethylene are observed to give nearly the same distribution coefficient. The area of heterogeneity increases and the acid increasingly favours the aqueous phase in the order, benzene, toluene, cyclohexene, cyclohexane and ‘hexane’. With ‘hexane’ and tetrachloroethylene as solvents, a comparison of the acetic and propionic acid systems indicates that the two‐phase region diminishes and shows that the acid decreasingly prefers the aqueous layer with increasing molecular weight of the acid. The tie‐line data for all the systems have been analysed by the correlation methods of Othmer—Tobias, Hand, and Bachman, and fitted satisfactorily by the corresponding equations.
